purpose: Border Gateway Protocol version 4 -- path-vector EGP for inter-AS routing and NLRI exchange

states.name: IDLE
states.description: Not attempting to connect; initial state or after an error resets the FSM. The BGP process is waiting for a ManualStart or AutomaticStart event before initiating a TCP connection to the peer. In SONiC/FRR, bgpd enters this state after a NOTIFICATION is received, too many retries, or on explicit 'neighbor <ip> shutdown'.

states.name: CONNECT
states.description: Waiting for the TCP three-way handshake to complete. bgpd has initiated a TCP SYN to the peer on port 179 and the ConnectRetry timer is running. If the TCP connection completes, the FSM moves to OPENSENT. If it fails, the FSM falls back to ACTIVE.

states.name: ACTIVE
states.description: TCP connection failed; retrying. Despite its misleading name, ACTIVE is a failure indicator -- bgpd is actively trying (and failing) to establish a TCP session to the peer. The ConnectRetry timer controls re-attempt intervals. In SONiC this is the most common symptom of IP reachability or ACL issues.

states.name: OPENSENT
states.description: TCP connection is up; a BGP OPEN message has been sent to the peer and bgpd is waiting for the peer's OPEN in return. The OPEN carries the local AS number, hold-time, BGP Identifier (router-id), and capability options. A Hold Timer (large value, typically 4 minutes per RFC) is started.

states.name: OPENCONFIRM
states.description: A valid OPEN has been received from the peer and a KEEPALIVE has been sent locally. The FSM is waiting for the peer's KEEPALIVE (or NOTIFICATION). Upon receiving KEEPALIVE the session moves to ESTABLISHED. A NOTIFICATION or expired Hold Timer returns to IDLE.

states.name: ESTABLISHED
states.description: Session is fully up. UPDATE, KEEPALIVE, and ROUTE-REFRESH messages are exchanged. Routes are being advertised, received, and installed into the FRR RIB. This is the only steady-state operational state. In SONiC, routes then flow bgpd -> zebra -> fpmsyncd -> APPL_DB -> orchagent -> ASIC_DB -> syncd.

transitions.trigger: ManualStart or AutomaticStart event; bgpd initiates TCP connection to peer on port 179 and starts ConnectRetry timer
TCP connection completes (three-way handshake done); bgpd sends OPEN message and starts Hold Timer
TCP connection failed or ConnectRetry timer expired; bgpd falls back to ACTIVE to listen for passive open
transitions.failure_if_stuck: Peer IP unreachable, TCP/179 blocked by iptables/nftables, wrong update-source, or MTU mismatch preventing TCP SYN

transitions.trigger: Passive TCP open succeeds (peer connects to us) or new active TCP attempt succeeds; OPEN message sent
ConnectRetry timer expires; bgpd retries active TCP connection to peer
Too many connection failures, idle-hold timer running, or neighbor shutdown
transitions.failure_if_stuck: Persistent reachability problem; check IP connectivity and container health (is bgpd running?)

transitions.trigger: Valid OPEN received from peer; bgpd sends KEEPALIVE and negotiates hold-time and capabilities
TCP session reset or collision detection; bgpd falls back to retry
Invalid OPEN received (NOTIFICATION sent) -- parameter mismatch: AS number, BGP version, router-id collision, capability negotiation failure, or MD5 TCP-AO authentication failure
transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check: remote AS config, BGP version, duplicate router-IDs, MD5 password mismatch

transitions.trigger: KEEPALIVE received from peer; negotiated hold-time set, session declared up
NOTIFICATION received or Hold Timer expired waiting for KEEPALIVE
transitions.failure_if_stuck: MD5 authentication mismatch or one-sided capability rejection; check 'vtysh -c show bgp neighbors' for notification reason

transitions.trigger: NOTIFICATION received (error in UPDATE, max-prefix exceeded, etc.), Hold Timer expired (missing KEEPALIVEs), TCP session lost, or 'clear bgp' command issued

timers.name: ConnectRetry
timers.description: Time between TCP connection attempts when in CONNECT or ACTIVE state. Restarted on each connection failure.
timers.sonic_config_path: CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR|<ip>: -- not directly configurable via sonic-cfggen; set via vtysh 'timers connect <seconds>' under neighbor
timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster new session establishment in lab; FRR default of 10s is reasonable for production

timers.name: Hold Timer
timers.description: Maximum time between received KEEPALIVE or UPDATE messages before declaring the peer dead. Negotiated in OPEN: the smaller value of the two peers' proposed hold-times is used. When it expires bgpd sends NOTIFICATION (Hold Timer Expired) and tears down the session.
timers.default: 180s (negotiated; RFC recommends peer proposes 90s, FRR default is 180s)
timers.on_expiry: Peer declared dead; NOTIFICATION Cease/Hold Timer Expired sent; session torn down; routes withdrawn from RIB
timers.sonic_config_path: CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR|<ip>: holdtime field, or BGP_GLOBALS timers_holdtime
timers.tuning_advice: Use BFD for sub-second failure detection rather than aggressive hold-timers. For data-center eBGP, 9s hold / 3s keepalive is common. 0 disables but risks never detecting dead peer.

timers.name: Keepalive Timer
timers.description: Interval at which bgpd sends KEEPALIVE messages to the peer to reset the peer's Hold Timer. Automatically calculated as hold-time / 3. Not independently configurable in most FRR deployments.
timers.sonic_config_path: CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR|<ip>: keepalive field, or BGP_GLOBALS timers_keepalive
timers.tuning_advice: Set via 'timers <keepalive> <holdtime>' under 'router bgp' or per-neighbor. Common for DC fabrics: 'timers 3 9'.

timers.name: MRAI (MinRouteAdvertisementInterval)
timers.description: Minimum time between consecutive UPDATEs to the same peer for the same prefix. Batches rapid route changes to prevent UPDATE storms. eBGP default is 30s, iBGP default is 5s. FRR SONiC deployments commonly set this to 0 for fast convergence.
timers.on_expiry: Pending batched UPDATEs are sent to the peer
timers.sonic_default: 0s in most SONiC deployments (advertisement-interval 0 is common in bgpcfgd templates)
timers.tuning_advice: Set to 0 for data-center fabrics requiring fast convergence. Keep default for WAN peering to protect against UPDATE storms from unstable peers.

timers.name: Idle Hold Timer
timers.description: After a session is reset due to an error (e.g., NOTIFICATION received), bgpd may delay before attempting to reconnect. The idle-hold timer enforces a back-off window. FRR implements exponential back-off on repeated failures.
timers.default: 5s initial; FRR doubles on repeated failures up to 120s
timers.on_expiry: BGP allowed to move from IDLE to CONNECT and attempt reconnection
timers.sonic_config_path: Not directly configurable via CONFIG_DB; controlled by FRR back-off logic
timers.tuning_advice: Not tunable in standard SONiC. If a peer is oscillating, the back-off dampens reconnect attempts automatically.

messages.name: OPEN
messages.description: Establishes BGP session parameters. The first message sent after TCP connects. Both peers exchange OPENs; mismatches result in NOTIFICATION and session teardown.
messages.when_sent: Immediately after TCP three-way handshake completes (CONNECT -> OPENSENT)
key_fields: Hold Time (proposed; negotiated to minimum of both peers)
BGP Identifier (router-id -- must be unique per peer)

Optional Parameters: Capabilities TLVs -- MP-BGP AFI/SAFI (RFC 4760), 4-byte ASN (RFC 6793), Route Refresh (RFC 2918), Graceful Restart (RFC 4724), ADD-PATH (RFC 7911), Enhanced Route Refresh (RFC 7313)

messages.name: UPDATE
messages.description: Advertises new routes (NLRIs) and/or withdraws previously advertised routes. The core BGP message for routing information exchange. After session establishment, bgpd sends the full RIB as UPDATEs; thereafter only incremental changes are sent.
messages.when_sent: During initial table exchange after ESTABLISHED; subsequently whenever routing information changes (new route, withdrawal, attribute change)
key_fields: Withdrawn Routes (list of prefixes being removed)

Path Attributes: ORIGIN (IGP/EGP/INCOMPLETE), AS_PATH, NEXT_HOP, MULTI_EXIT_DISC (MED), LOCAL_PREF (iBGP only), ATOMIC_AGGREGATE, AGGREGATOR, COMMUNITY (RFC 1997), LARGE_COMMUNITY (RFC 8092), EXTENDED_COMMUNITY (RFC 4360), MP_REACH_NLRI (RFC 4760), MP_UNREACH_NLRI (RFC 4760)
NLRI (Network Layer Reachability Information -- list of prefixes being advertised)

messages.name: KEEPALIVE
messages.description: Maintains session liveness by resetting the peer's Hold Timer. Contains only the 19-byte BGP common header (no body). Also used to acknowledge the peer's OPEN during the OPENCONFIRM state.
messages.when_sent: Every keepalive-interval (default 60s, derived as hold-time/3); also once during OPENCONFIRM to acknowledge the peer's OPEN
key_fields: (19-byte BGP common header only: Marker=0xFF*16, Length=19, Type=4)

messages.name: NOTIFICATION
messages.description: Reports a protocol error and terminates the session. After sending NOTIFICATION, bgpd closes the TCP connection and returns to IDLE. The error code and subcode identify the exact cause.
messages.when_sent: Whenever a BGP error is detected: OPEN parameter mismatch, invalid UPDATE, Hold Timer expiry, maximum-prefix exceeded, finite state machine error, administrative reset
key_fields: Error Code: 1=Message Header Error, 2=OPEN Message Error, 3=UPDATE Message Error, 4=Hold Timer Expired, 5=Finite State Machine Error, 6=Cease

Error Subcode: further qualifies the error (e.g., Cease/6.1=Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached, Cease/6.2=Administrative Shutdown, Cease/6.3=Peer De-configured, Cease/6.4=Administrative Reset, Cease/6.8=Hard Reset)
Data: variable-length field containing the offending value or attribute

messages.name: ROUTE-REFRESH
messages.description: Requests the peer to re-advertise its full BGP table for a specific AFI/SAFI without tearing down the session. Used after an inbound policy change to re-apply the new policy to routes already received. Requires the Route Refresh capability to be negotiated in OPEN.
messages.when_sent: Triggered by operator ('clear bgp <ip> soft in') or automatically after inbound policy change if soft-reconfiguration is not configured

failure_modes.scenario: Session stuck in ACTIVE or CONNECT -- TCP not establishing
failure_modes.description: bgpd cannot complete the TCP three-way handshake to the peer on port 179. The session oscillates between CONNECT and ACTIVE states indefinitely. In SONiC this is the most common BGP failure and almost always means a Layer 3 reachability or firewall problem -- not a BGP protocol issue.
symptoms: 'show bgp summary' shows state Active or Connect for the neighbor
Session uptime shows 'never' or resets frequently
'sudo ss -tnp | grep 179' shows no ESTABLISHED connection to peer IP
bgpd logs show repeated 'bgpd[]: %ADJCHANGE: ... Active' or connection refused messages
root_causes: Peer IP address unreachable -- no route to peer, wrong IP configured
iptables/nftables rule on the SONiC host blocking TCP port 179 (check 'sudo iptables -L -n | grep 179')
BGP container not running or bgpd process crashed inside bgp container
Incorrect update-source -- bgpd is sourcing TCP from the wrong interface/IP
eBGP multihop not configured when peer is more than 1 hop away (TTL=1 default for eBGP)
MD5 TCP authentication mismatch -- peer sends RST on SYN with wrong or missing MD5 signature
MTU mismatch -- TCP SYN succeeds but large BGP OPENs are fragmented/dropped
Wrong remote-as configured -- peer rejects the connection at the TCP level or sends NOTIFICATION
resolution: Ping peer IP from SONiC: 'ping <peer-ip> -I <src-interface>'
Check iptables: 'sudo iptables -L -n | grep 179' and 'sudo ip6tables -L -n | grep 179'
Check bgp container: 'docker ps | grep bgp' and 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show bgp summary'
Verify update-source: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip>"' -- check 'Local host'
For multihop eBGP: add 'neighbor <ip> ebgp-multihop <ttl>' in FRR config
Verify MD5 password matches on both ends under neighbor config
Check MTU: 'ip link show <interface>' and compare with peer

verify_commands.look_for: Neighbor state column -- Active or Connect indicates TCP not up
verify_commands.confirms_if: Shows Active or Connect state for the peer

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip>'
verify_commands.look_for: BGP state field, Last error, Local host/port, Foreign host/port
verify_commands.confirms_if: Shows detailed FSM state and local source IP being used

verify_commands.look_for: ESTAB entries on port 179 to the peer IP; SYN-SENT indicates TCP not completing
verify_commands.confirms_if: No ESTAB entry to peer IP confirms TCP is not connecting

verify_commands.look_for: Packet loss -- any loss indicates IP reachability problem
verify_commands.confirms_if: 100% packet loss confirms the peer IP is unreachable from this source

verify_commands.look_for: DROP or REJECT rules matching port 179
verify_commands.confirms_if: Matching DROP rule on port 179 confirms firewall is blocking BGP TCP

verify_commands.look_for: bgp container status -- should show 'Up' not 'Exited'
verify_commands.confirms_if: Exited or missing bgp container confirms BGP daemon is not running

failure_modes.scenario: Session flapping -- ESTABLISHED cycling to IDLE repeatedly
failure_modes.description: The BGP session reaches ESTABLISHED but drops and re-establishes repeatedly. Each flap triggers route withdrawals and re-advertisements that propagate instability upstream and downstream. In SONiC the most common causes are Hold Timer expiry (CPU-bound bgpd or high-latency path) and underlying link or tunnel instability.
symptoms: 'show bgp summary' shows short uptime (seconds to minutes) for the neighbor
syslog shows repeated '%ADJCHANGE: neighbor <ip> Down' and 'Up' entries
Downstream peers receive repeated prefix withdrawals and re-advertisements
'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip>"' shows high Connections dropped count
root_causes: Hold Timer expiry: bgpd not sending KEEPALIVEs within hold-time due to CPU overload or I/O stall in bgp container
Underlying link or tunnel flapping (the IP path to the peer goes down briefly)
NOTIFICATION from peer: max-prefix limit exceeded, capability mismatch on re-negotiation, MD5 change
bgp container OOM killed by Linux kernel -- bgpd restarts and sessions reset
BFD failure declaring peer dead faster than BGP timers (correct behavior, but may look like BGP flap)
FRR bgpd bug causing crash-restart cycle (check 'docker inspect bgp' for restart count)
resolution: Check last NOTIFICATION reason: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip>"' -- look for Last error
Check bgp container resource usage: 'docker stats bgp'
Check bgpd crash/restart: 'docker inspect bgp | grep RestartCount'
Check system syslog: 'sudo tail -f /var/log/syslog | grep -E "BGP|bgpd"'
If Hold Timer expiry: either increase timers ('timers 10 30') or reduce bgpd CPU load
If BFD-triggered: investigate the underlay path stability
Check kernel OOM: 'sudo dmesg | grep -i oom | tail -20'

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip>'
verify_commands.look_for: Last error field, Connections established/dropped counts, hold time, last reset reason
verify_commands.confirms_if: High Connections dropped count and short uptime confirm flapping; Last error field identifies cause

verify_commands.look_for: Repeated Up/Down transitions with timestamps; NOTIFICATION error codes
verify_commands.confirms_if: Repeated adjacency change log messages confirm flapping with timing pattern

verify_commands.look_for: CPU % and MEM USAGE -- high CPU in bgp container can cause KEEPALIVE misses
verify_commands.confirms_if: CPU above 80% in bgp container confirms KEEPALIVE suppression by CPU overload

verify_commands.look_for: RestartCount > 0 indicates bgpd container has been restarted
verify_commands.confirms_if: Non-zero RestartCount confirms bgpd has crashed and been restarted

verify_commands.look_for: OOM kill events targeting bgpd or the bgp container cgroup
verify_commands.confirms_if: OOM kill event for bgpd confirms memory pressure is crashing the session

failure_modes.scenario: Routes not being advertised to peer
failure_modes.description: The BGP session is ESTABLISHED but the peer receives zero or fewer prefixes than expected from this SONiC device. Routes exist in the local FRR BGP table but are not being sent. Almost always a policy, address-family activation, or next-hop issue.
symptoms: Peer reports 0 prefixes received from this router
'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip> advertised-routes"' returns empty or partial list
Routes visible in 'show bgp ipv4 unicast' locally but not on remote end
No UPDATE messages seen in 'sudo tcpdump -i any tcp port 179' after session establishes
root_causes: Address family not activated for the neighbor (must add 'neighbor <ip> activate' under address-family ipv4 unicast in FRR)
Outbound route-map or prefix-list denying all or some routes
No 'network' statement and no redistribution configured -- nothing to advertise
Route not selected as best path by BGP best-path algorithm (only best paths are advertised)
next-hop-self not set for iBGP -- eBGP next-hop is not reachable by iBGP peers
bgpcfgd template error: outbound policy applied that was not intended
Peer is in a different address family (e.g., IPv6 unicast) and IPv4 unicast is the active one
resolution: Check address-family activation: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip>"' -- look for 'AF activated' lines
Check outbound policy: look for 'route-map' or 'prefix-list' in FRR neighbor config
Verify network statements: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show running-config"' under 'router bgp'
Check best path: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp ipv4 unicast <prefix>"' -- routes without '>' are not best and won't be advertised
After policy change, soft reset: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "clear bgp <ip> soft out"'

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip> advertised-routes'
verify_commands.look_for: List of prefixes being sent to this peer; empty means nothing is being advertised
verify_commands.confirms_if: Empty output confirms routes are not being advertised to this specific peer

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp ipv4 unicast'
verify_commands.look_for: '>' (best path) marker on routes -- only best-path routes are advertised; routes without '>' will not be sent
verify_commands.confirms_if: Routes without '>' marker will not be advertised regardless of policy

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip>'
verify_commands.look_for: Address-family activation status, outbound route-map name, community-list filters
verify_commands.confirms_if: Shows exactly which outbound policy is applied to this neighbor and which AFIs are activated

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip> policy'
verify_commands.look_for: Outbound route-map and prefix-list names applied to the neighbor
verify_commands.confirms_if: Presence of an outbound policy name indicates filtering is in place; check that policy for deny clauses

failure_modes.scenario: Routes received from peer but not installed in Linux kernel RIB
failure_modes.description: BGP session is ESTABLISHED and 'show bgp ipv4 unicast' shows routes from the peer with the '>' best-path marker, but 'show ip route' does not have these routes and traffic is not forwarded. The route is stuck in the FRR BGP table and never reaches zebra.
symptoms: 'show bgp ipv4 unicast' shows route with '>' (best) but 'show ip route' does not have the prefix
Traffic to the destination black-holes despite BGP showing the route
'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show ip route <prefix>"' returns no entry
fpmsyncd logs may show no activity for the affected prefix
root_causes: BGP next-hop unreachable from zebra's perspective -- next-hop not in the kernel routing table
iBGP peer without next-hop-self -- the eBGP next-hop is not reachable by the internal router
VRF mismatch -- route learned in default VRF but needs to be in a management or data VRF
FRR zebra not running or unresponsive -- bgpd cannot push routes to kernel
Maximum prefix import limit in zebra (rarely hit but possible on large tables)
BFD declaring next-hop unreachable even though BGP session is up (path asymmetry)
resolution: Check next-hop reachability: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show ip route <next-hop-ip>"'
For iBGP: add 'neighbor <ip> next-hop-self' on the border router
Check zebra: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show ip route"' -- if zebra has no routes at all, zebra may be down
Check FRR process health: 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl status'
Check bgpd -> zebra nexthop tracking: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp nexthop"'

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp ipv4 unicast <prefix>'
verify_commands.look_for: Next-hop address and whether it is marked 'not accessible' or 'inaccessible'; '>' indicates best but zebra may still reject it
verify_commands.confirms_if: Route in BGP table with '>' but not in 'show ip route' confirms BGP-to-RIB installation failure

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp nexthop'
verify_commands.look_for: Next-hop reachability status column -- 'reachable' vs 'unreachable'; IGP metric
verify_commands.confirms_if: Unreachable next-hop in nexthop table confirms why the route is not installed in the RIB

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show ip route <next-hop-ip>'
verify_commands.look_for: Whether a route to the BGP next-hop exists in the kernel/zebra RIB
verify_commands.confirms_if: No route to next-hop confirms the root cause -- zebra cannot install the BGP route

verify_commands.look_for: All FRR processes (bgpd, zebra, staticd) should show RUNNING
verify_commands.confirms_if: zebra in STOPPED or FATAL state explains why no routes are installed

failure_modes.scenario: Routes in Linux kernel RIB but not programmed into ASIC hardware
failure_modes.description: Routes exist in the Linux kernel routing table ('show ip route bgp' shows them) and in FRR, but traffic is still not forwarded -- the routes were never pushed into the ASIC forwarding table. The failure is in the SONiC pipeline: fpmsyncd -> APPL_DB -> orchagent -> ASIC_DB -> syncd. This is the most SONiC-specific BGP failure mode.
symptoms: 'show ip route bgp' shows the prefix in the kernel RIB
'show route-map' shows no filtering
Traffic to prefix is dropped or not forwarded by ASIC
APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE may have the entry but ASIC_DB does not
orchagent logs show SAI errors: 'ERR orchagent: Failed to create/set route entry'
syncd logs show SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL or SAI_STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES
root_causes: fpmsyncd not running or failed to push route to APPL_DB -- check fpmsyncd health
orchagent failed to translate APPL_DB route to SAI call -- check orchagent logs for SAI errors
ASIC FIB/LPM table full -- TCAM or LPM resource exhaustion; SAI returns table-full error
syncd crashed or is in error state -- ASIC_DB entries queued but not programmed
Next-hop neighbor (ARP/ND) not resolved -- orchagent waiting for neighbor entry before installing route
ECMP group limit exceeded on the ASIC
Route stuck in APPL_DB without reaching ASIC_DB -- orchagent dependency on another table (e.g., nexthop group)
resolution: Check APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE: 'redis-cli -n 0 HGETALL "ROUTE_TABLE:<prefix>"'
Check ASIC_DB for route: 'redis-cli -n 1 KEYS "ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ROUTE_ENTRY*" | grep <prefix>'
Check orchagent logs: 'docker logs swss 2>&1 | grep -E "ERROR|WARN|route" | tail -50'
Check syncd logs: 'docker logs syncd 2>&1 | grep -E "ERROR|SAI_STATUS" | tail -50'
Check FIB resource limits: 'show system resources' or platform-specific ASIC capacity
Verify fpmsyncd is running: 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd'
Restart fpmsyncd if stuck: 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl restart fpmsyncd' (non-disruptive to BGP sessions)

verify_commands.look_for: Prefix present in kernel RIB with BGP source code 'B'; confirms route made it out of FRR but need to verify ASIC programming
verify_commands.confirms_if: Route in kernel RIB confirms FRR->kernel path is working; absence of route in ASIC confirms pipeline failure downstream

verify_commands.look_for: nexthop, ifname fields present in APPL_DB; absence means fpmsyncd has not published the route
verify_commands.confirms_if: Empty result means fpmsyncd failed to write to APPL_DB; populated result means failure is in orchagent or syncd

verify_commands.look_for: Matching key for the prefix; absence means orchagent or syncd has not programmed the route into ASIC_DB
verify_commands.confirms_if: Key absent in ASIC_DB confirms route was not programmed into hardware

verify_commands.look_for: SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL, SAI_STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES, or 'Failed to create route entry' errors
verify_commands.confirms_if: SAI error messages in orchagent logs confirm ASIC programming failure with specific error code

verify_commands.look_for: SAI API call failures or status codes indicating ASIC errors
verify_commands.confirms_if: SAI errors in syncd confirm the ASIC rejected the route programming

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd
verify_commands.look_for: fpmsyncd process status -- should be RUNNING
verify_commands.confirms_if: fpmsyncd in STOPPED or FATAL state confirms the APPL_DB publishing step is broken

failure_modes.scenario: BGP configuration not taking effect -- bgpcfgd not syncing
failure_modes.description: An operator has modified BGP configuration via 'config bgp' CLI commands or by directly editing CONFIG_DB, but the changes do not appear in FRR ('docker exec bgp vtysh -c show running-config' shows the old config). bgpcfgd is the daemon that translates CONFIG_DB -> FRR vtysh commands via Jinja2 templates. If bgpcfgd is stuck, crashed, or misconfigured, CONFIG_DB changes are silently not applied.
symptoms: CONFIG_DB shows the new neighbor or timer configuration
'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show running-config' does NOT show the new config
New BGP neighbor never comes up despite being configured via CLI
Timer or policy changes configured via 'config bgp' have no effect on FRR behavior
bgpcfgd logs show template rendering errors or database subscription failures
root_causes: bgpcfgd process crashed or exited inside the bgp container
CONFIG_DB entry has a malformed key or missing required field that causes bgpcfgd template rendering to fail silently
bgpcfgd Jinja2 template bug -- certain config combinations cause a rendering exception
CONFIG_DB change made via direct redis-cli write that bypassed validation -- malformed JSON or key format
bgpcfgd is running but the CONFIG_DB subscriber thread lost the connection (Redis subscription dropped)
Direct vtysh changes were made previously and conflict with what bgpcfgd tries to apply (bgpcfgd will overwrite on next config push)
resolution: Check bgpcfgd status: 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl status bgpcfgd'
Check bgpcfgd logs: 'docker exec bgp cat /var/log/bgpcfgd.log' or 'docker logs bgp 2>&1 | grep bgpcfgd'
Verify CONFIG_DB entry: 'redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL "BGP_NEIGHBOR|<ip>"'
Restart bgpcfgd: 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl restart bgpcfgd' (will re-read all CONFIG_DB and push to FRR)
If CONFIG_DB is malformed: fix via 'config bgp ...' CLI, not direct redis-cli writes
After bgpcfgd restart, confirm: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show running-config' shows the expected neighbor

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status bgpcfgd
verify_commands.look_for: bgpcfgd should be RUNNING; STOPPED or FATAL means it crashed
verify_commands.confirms_if: bgpcfgd not in RUNNING state confirms config sync is broken

verify_commands.command: docker logs bgp 2>&1 | grep -i bgpcfgd | tail -30
verify_commands.look_for: Template rendering errors, exception tracebacks, or Redis connection errors
verify_commands.confirms_if: Error messages in bgpcfgd output confirm config sync failure and may identify the specific template or key causing the issue

verify_commands.look_for: rrclient, name, local_addr, nhopself, holdtime, keepalive, asn, admin_status fields
verify_commands.confirms_if: Correct values in CONFIG_DB confirm the problem is in bgpcfgd sync (not in the config itself); missing fields indicate a bad config write

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep -A5 'neighbor <ip>'
verify_commands.look_for: Whether the neighbor appears in FRR running config with the expected parameters
verify_commands.confirms_if: Absence of neighbor in FRR running-config despite CONFIG_DB having it confirms bgpcfgd sync failure

failure_modes.scenario: Direct vtysh config overwritten by bgpcfgd on restart
failure_modes.description: An operator made BGP configuration changes directly via 'docker exec bgp vtysh' without updating CONFIG_DB. The changes worked temporarily. After bgpcfgd restarts (due to container restart, config reload, or bgpcfgd crash-recovery), it re-applies CONFIG_DB to FRR and overwrites the manual vtysh changes. This is a SONiC-specific human error pattern -- vtysh is not the source of truth in SONiC.
symptoms: BGP configuration change worked after 'docker exec bgp vtysh' but disappeared after container restart or 'config reload'
'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show running-config' shows config that does not match what was manually entered
Neighbor that was manually added is gone after restart
Timer or policy manually set via vtysh reverted to old values
root_causes: Operator used 'docker exec bgp vtysh' to configure BGP directly, bypassing CONFIG_DB
bgpcfgd controls FRR config and re-applies CONFIG_DB state on every restart, overwriting any manual changes
FRR integrated config mode not enabled -- /etc/frr/bgpd.conf is generated by bgpcfgd from templates, not written directly
Operator unaware that SONiC architecture separates configuration source of truth (CONFIG_DB) from FRR runtime config
resolution: Always use SONiC CLI ('config bgp ...') to make persistent BGP configuration changes
Check what CONFIG_DB has: 'redis-cli -n 4 KEYS "BGP*"' to see all BGP-related CONFIG_DB tables
Add missing config via SONiC CLI: 'config bgp neighbor <ip> ...'
Save config: 'config save' to persist CONFIG_DB to /etc/sonic/config_db.json
Verify FRR picked it up: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show running-config' after bgpcfgd processes the change

verify_commands.look_for: BGP_NEIGHBOR, BGP_GLOBALS, BGP_PEER_RANGE entries -- these are the source of truth bgpcfgd uses
verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing entry in CONFIG_DB for a neighbor that exists in vtysh confirms the config was made directly in vtysh

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep -c 'neighbor'
verify_commands.look_for: Number of neighbor entries in FRR vs number of BGP_NEIGHBOR entries in CONFIG_DB
verify_commands.confirms_if: More neighbors in FRR than CONFIG_DB confirms manual vtysh additions that will be lost on restart

verify_commands.look_for: router_id, local_asn, and global BGP parameters
verify_commands.confirms_if: Shows the CONFIG_DB source of truth for global BGP parameters; discrepancy with FRR running-config confirms drift

failure_modes.scenario: BGP routes received but ECMP paths not installed in hardware
failure_modes.description: BGP has multiple equal-cost paths to a prefix (ECMP) but only one path (or none) appears in 'show ip route'. Either BGP multipath is not configured in FRR, or orchagent failed to create the ECMP nexthop group in the ASIC. In data-center spine-leaf fabrics running SONiC with eBGP underlay, ECMP is critical for full bandwidth utilization.
symptoms: 'show bgp ipv4 unicast <prefix>' shows multiple paths with '*' (multipath) marker but 'show ip route' shows only one nexthop
Traffic is not load-balanced across fabric links
ASIC_DB shows only one nexthop for the route entry where multiple are expected
orchagent logs show 'Failed to create nexthop group' or ECMP group count exceeded
root_causes: BGP multipath not configured in FRR -- 'maximum-paths <n>' not set under address-family
bgpcfgd template does not include maximum-paths in its FRR config output
ECMP nexthop group limit exceeded on the ASIC (platform-specific limit -- e.g., 4K or 16K groups)
AS path length differs between paths and 'bestpath as-path multipath-relax' is not configured for eBGP ECMP in spine-leaf
orchagent SAI error creating ECMP group -- ASIC resource exhaustion
resolution: Configure multipath in FRR: 'maximum-paths <n>' and 'maximum-paths ibgp <n>' under 'address-family ipv4 unicast'
For eBGP spine-leaf ECMP: add 'bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax' under 'router bgp'
Check ECMP group count: 'redis-cli -n 1 KEYS "ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEXT_HOP_GROUP*" | wc -l'
Check orchagent logs for nexthop group errors: 'docker logs swss 2>&1 | grep -i "nexthop group" | tail -30'

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp ipv4 unicast <prefix>'
verify_commands.look_for: '*' (multipath) marker on multiple paths; absence of '*' means BGP is not selecting multiple paths
verify_commands.confirms_if: Only one path marked '>' with no '*' paths confirms multipath is not configured or not eligible

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep maximum-paths
verify_commands.look_for: 'maximum-paths' setting under address-family; absent means ECMP is disabled
verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing maximum-paths confirms BGP multipath is not configured in FRR

verify_commands.look_for: Total count of ECMP groups in ASIC_DB; compare against platform ECMP group limit
verify_commands.confirms_if: Count at or near platform limit confirms ECMP group resource exhaustion

verify_commands.command: docker logs swss 2>&1 | grep -i 'nexthop group' | tail -30
verify_commands.look_for: SAI errors creating nexthop groups; SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL or similar
verify_commands.confirms_if: SAI nexthop group creation errors confirm ASIC ECMP resource exhaustion

failure_modes.scenario: fpmsyncd not forwarding routes from FRR kernel to APPL_DB
failure_modes.description: FRR (zebra) has installed routes into the Linux kernel routing table but fpmsyncd, which monitors the kernel netlink socket for route changes and publishes them to APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE, has stalled or disconnected. Routes exist in 'ip route show' output but never appear in APPL_DB and therefore never reach orchagent or the ASIC.
symptoms: 'ip route show' (inside bgp container or host) shows BGP routes in kernel
APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE is empty or missing the expected prefixes: 'redis-cli -n 0 KEYS "ROUTE_TABLE*"'
fpmsyncd process shows as RUNNING but has not updated APPL_DB recently
No orchagent or syncd activity for the affected prefixes
Routes that appeared briefly in APPL_DB are now stale/missing after fpmsyncd restart
root_causes: fpmsyncd lost its netlink socket connection to the kernel and did not reconnect
fpmsyncd lost its Redis connection to APPL_DB
FPM (Forwarding Plane Manager) protocol socket between zebra and fpmsyncd is broken
fpmsyncd crashed and restarted, flushing APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE entries it owned but not yet re-syncing
zebra FPM module not loaded or FPM connection not established (check 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show zebra fpm stats')
resolution: Check fpmsyncd: 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd'
Check fpmsyncd logs: 'docker logs bgp 2>&1 | grep fpmsyncd | tail -30'
Verify FPM connection: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show zebra fpm stats"' -- should show connected and messages sent
Restart fpmsyncd (safe, does not affect BGP sessions): 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl restart fpmsyncd'
After restart, fpmsyncd re-syncs kernel routes to APPL_DB; verify: 'redis-cli -n 0 KEYS "ROUTE_TABLE*" | wc -l'

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd
verify_commands.look_for: fpmsyncd process state -- should be RUNNING
verify_commands.confirms_if: STOPPED or FATAL state confirms fpmsyncd is not publishing routes

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show zebra fpm stats'
verify_commands.look_for: Connection status (connected/disconnected), messages sent count, last update time
verify_commands.confirms_if: Disconnected or zero messages sent confirms FPM socket is broken between zebra and fpmsyncd

verify_commands.look_for: Count of route entries in APPL_DB; should be non-zero if BGP routes are installed in kernel
verify_commands.confirms_if: Zero entries when kernel has BGP routes confirms fpmsyncd is not publishing to APPL_DB

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp ip route show proto bgp | wc -l
verify_commands.look_for: Count of BGP routes in the Linux kernel routing table inside the bgp container
verify_commands.confirms_if: Non-zero count in kernel but zero in APPL_DB confirms the fpmsyncd pipeline failure

failure_modes.scenario: BGP session stuck in OPENSENT -- OPEN parameter mismatch
failure_modes.description: The TCP connection succeeds and bgpd sends its OPEN message, but the session never advances to OPENCONFIRM or ESTABLISHED. Instead it returns to ACTIVE or IDLE. The cause is a BGP OPEN message parameter mismatch: the peer sends NOTIFICATION and rejects the OPEN. This generates a Notification message with an OPEN Message Error code.
symptoms: Session briefly reaches OPENSENT or OPENCONFIRM then drops back to ACTIVE/IDLE
'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip>"' shows 'Last error: Open Message Error' or 'Bad Peer AS' or 'Unacceptable Hold Time'
tcpdump on port 179 shows BGP NOTIFICATION packet with error code 2 (OPEN Message Error)
Session never stays in OPENSENT/OPENCONFIRM for more than a second
root_causes: Remote AS number mismatch: 'neighbor <ip> remote-as <N>' does not match peer's actual AS
Router-ID collision: both sides have the same BGP router-id and collision detection triggers rejection
Hold time incompatibility: peer proposes hold-time < 3 seconds (RFC minimum) or other end rejects the proposed value
4-byte ASN capability not supported by peer but local bgpd is advertising it
MD5 TCP-AO authentication: TCP session has MD5 but session makes it to OPEN stage with wrong hash
VRF mismatch: neighbor configured in wrong VRF context in FRR
AFI/SAFI capability mismatch causing one side to send NOTIFICATION
resolution: Check last NOTIFICATION: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors <ip>"' -- Last error and Last error subcode fields
Capture OPEN message: 'sudo tcpdump -i any tcp port 179 -c 50 -w /tmp/bgp.pcap' then inspect OPENs
Verify remote-as: confirm the peer's configured ASN matches 'neighbor <ip> remote-as' in CONFIG_DB
Check router-ids: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp"' -- compare router-id on both ends
If hold-time issue: configure compatible 'timers <keepalive> <holdtime>' on both sides

verify_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip>'
verify_commands.look_for: Last error field: error code and subcode (e.g., 'OPEN Message Error/Bad Peer AS', 'Hold Timer Expired', 'Unacceptable Hold Time')
verify_commands.confirms_if: OPEN Message Error in Last error field confirms parameter mismatch at session negotiation

verify_commands.look_for: BGP NOTIFICATION packet with error code 2 (OPEN Message Error); direction shows which side is rejecting
verify_commands.confirms_if: NOTIFICATION from peer with error code 2 confirms peer rejected our OPEN parameters

verify_commands.look_for: asn field in CONFIG_DB for this neighbor
verify_commands.confirms_if: Correct ASN in CONFIG_DB vs actual peer ASN -- mismatch confirms wrong remote-as configuration

dependencies.name: TCP connectivity on port 179
dependencies.description: BGP requires an established TCP session to the peer on port 179. In SONiC the bgp container must have IP reachability to the peer and no iptables/nftables rules blocking TCP/179.

dependencies.name: FRR stack (bgpd + zebra)
dependencies.description: BGP in SONiC runs inside the 'bgp' Docker container via FRR. bgpd handles the BGP FSM and route selection. zebra maintains the kernel RIB and receives routes from bgpd via the FRR inter-process socket. Both must be running for BGP to function.

dependencies.name: bgpcfgd
dependencies.description: Translates CONFIG_DB BGP entries into FRR vtysh commands. Required for any CONFIG_DB-driven BGP configuration to reach FRR. Runs inside the bgp container alongside bgpd.

dependencies.name: fpmsyncd
dependencies.description: Subscribes to Linux kernel netlink events for route changes and publishes them to APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE. Without fpmsyncd, routes in the kernel RIB never reach orchagent or the ASIC. Runs inside the bgp container.

dependencies.name: orchagent (swss container)
dependencies.description: Consumes APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE entries and translates them into SAI API calls written to ASIC_DB. Routes are not programmed into hardware until orchagent processes them successfully.

dependencies.name: syncd (syncd container)
dependencies.description: Reads ASIC_DB and calls the vendor SAI library to program the physical ASIC forwarding tables. The final step in the route programming pipeline.

dependencies.name: CONFIG_DB (Redis DB 4)
dependencies.description: Source of truth for BGP configuration: BGP_NEIGHBOR, BGP_GLOBALS, BGP_PEER_RANGE tables. bgpcfgd reads from here to drive FRR configuration.

dependencies.name: APPL_DB (Redis DB 0)
dependencies.description: Route publication bus between fpmsyncd and orchagent. Routes must appear in ROUTE_TABLE here before they can reach the ASIC.

dependencies.name: BFD (optional)
dependencies.description: Bidirectional Forwarding Detection provides sub-second peer failure detection. When BFD declares a peer down, it triggers BGP session teardown faster than the Hold Timer. Configured as 'neighbor <ip> bfd' in FRR.

relevant_fields: holdtime: proposed hold timer in seconds
router_id: BGP router ID (must be unique)
load_balance_mp_relax: enable multipath-relax for eBGP ECMP (true/false)
ip_range: list of IP prefixes for dynamic neighbor matching
src_intf: source interface for this peer group
nexthop: comma-separated list of next-hop IPs (ECMP)
nexthop-vrf: VRF for the nexthop (for VRF route leak)

sonic_notes.note: BGP in SONiC runs inside the 'bgp' Docker container via FRR (Free Range Routing). The bgpd process handles the BGP FSM, best-path selection, and UPDATE processing. Zebra handles the kernel RIB. Do NOT confuse 'configure BGP' with 'edit vtysh directly' -- bgpcfgd controls the FRR configuration from CONFIG_DB.

bgpcfgd translates CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR, BGP_GLOBALS, and BGP_PEER_RANGE tables into FRR vtysh commands using Jinja2 templates. It subscribes to CONFIG_DB via redis pub/sub and re-renders on every change. Changes made directly via 'docker exec bgp vtysh' are NOT persisted and will be overwritten on the next bgpcfgd restart or config reload.

The BGP route programming pipeline in SONiC is: bgpd (BGP table) -> zebra (kernel RIB) -> fpmsyncd (APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE) -> orchagent (ASIC_DB SAI route objects) -> syncd (vendor SAI library) -> ASIC forwarding table. A failure at any stage in this pipeline stops routes from reaching hardware. Always check each stage when routes are in FRR but not forwarding.

fpmsyncd connects to zebra via the FPM (Forwarding Plane Manager) protocol, a lightweight socket protocol on localhost port 2620. If the FPM socket breaks (e.g., zebra restart), fpmsyncd must reconnect and resync. Check 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c show zebra fpm stats' to verify the connection is active. Restarting fpmsyncd ('docker exec bgp supervisorctl restart fpmsyncd') is safe and non-disruptive to BGP sessions.
sonic_notes.source_ref: sonic-buildimage:src/sonic-frr/ (fpmsyncd) and FRR zebra FPM module

sonic_notes.note: In SONiC, BGP configuration is not in /etc/frr/bgpd.conf as a static file in production. bgpcfgd generates and applies FRR config dynamically. The canonical BGP config lives in CONFIG_DB and is persisted to /etc/sonic/config_db.json via 'config save'. Always run 'config save' after BGP config changes or they will be lost on reboot.

SONiC uses FRR integrated configuration mode ('frr defaults traditional' or 'frr defaults datacenter') depending on the build. In 'datacenter' defaults mode, some BGP behaviors differ from traditional IOS-style FRR defaults: EBGP multihop is more permissive, log-adjacency-changes is on by default, and 'bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax' may be enabled by default in some SONiC distros.
sonic_notes.source_ref: FRR documentation: frr defaults and SONiC bgpcfgd template

sonic_notes.note: SONiC BGP does NOT use Cisco-style 'neighbor X route-reflector-client' CLI. Route reflector configuration is in CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR table as 'rrclient: true'. bgpcfgd translates this to 'neighbor <ip> route-reflector-client' in FRR. Forgetting this distinction when troubleshooting with vtysh-familiar engineers causes confusion.

BGP ECMP in SONiC spine-leaf fabrics requires 'load_balance_mp_relax: true' in BGP_GLOBALS|default (CONFIG_DB) which maps to 'bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax' in FRR. Without this, eBGP ECMP does not work because all spine paths have different AS_PATHs (each spine has a unique AS in typical BGP unnumbered or numbered underlay designs).
sonic_notes.source_ref: SONiC BGP design for ECMP and sonic-bgpcfgd globals template

sonic_notes.note: BGP unnumbered (RFC 5549) is widely used in SONiC data-center fabrics. In this model, peering is done over link-local IPv6 addresses without requiring explicit IP address configuration on each BGP neighbor. The neighbor is specified by interface name ('neighbor <ifname> interface remote-as external'). bgpcfgd supports this via BGP_PEER_RANGE with src_intf field. Ensure IPv6 link-local is enabled on the peering interface.
sonic_notes.source_ref: RFC 5549 and SONiC BGP unnumbered design

sonic_frr_mapping.frr_config_file: /etc/frr/bgpd.conf (generated by bgpcfgd; do not edit directly)
sonic_frr_mapping.config_managed_by: bgpcfgd (Jinja2 templates rendering CONFIG_DB into FRR vtysh commands)

key_commands: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp summary'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip>'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp ipv4 unicast'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp nexthop'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show zebra fpm stats'
docker logs bgp 2>&1 | grep -i bgpcfgd | tail -30
